summary: Provide a simple, responsive layout adjustment so the Analyze Deal button sits inline with the Address input on the Deals & Scenarios screen. Recommend wrapping the address field and button in a flex row with proper spacing and responsive stacking on small screens. No new dependencies; reuse existing design tokens/classes.
completed_work: Defined the minimal, framework-agnostic approach to align the Analyze Deal button inline with the Address input
Outlined responsive behavior (inline on desktop/tablet, stacked on small screens)
Specified accessibility, keyboard order, and spacing standards
Provided example CSS and Tailwind-class patterns to implement using existing styles
Listed risks and testing/acceptance criteria to validate the change
artifacts_updated: No repository artifacts updated in this stage
decision_or_outcome: Do not introduce new dependencies; use existing CSS/utilities
Wrap Address input and Analyze Deal button in a single row container using flex with gap and align-items to bottom or baseline to match input height
Make the input flex:1 so it grows; keep button intrinsic width; ensure min-width:0 on input wrapper to avoid overflow
On small screens, stack vertically with full-width button under the input
Preserve semantics, accessibility, and tab order; no behavioral changes to the button action
next_required_action: Implement the layout adjustment in the Deals & Scenarios screen using the recommended structure and open a PR for QA validation.
blockers_or_risks: Unknown UI framework/utility system in use; engineer should apply the closest equivalent using current project standards
Potential one-off styles on the screen may override alignment; confirm no regressions across breakpoints
Safari/Firefox baseline alignment quirks; verify align-items choice visually across major browsers

implementation_plan.goal: Align the Analyze Deal button horizontally with the Address input on the Deals & Scenarios screen, achieving a clean, intentional, and symmetric layout while preserving responsive behavior and accessibility.
implementation_plan.issue_body: Update the Deals & Scenarios screen so the blue “Analyze Deal” button is inline with the Address input field, appearing on the same row, with consistent spacing and alignment. On small screens, the elements should stack gracefully with the button full-width below the input. No behavioral or functional changes are required.
recommended_first_changes: Locate the Deals & Scenarios view/component (search for the button label 'Analyze Deal' and the Address input).
Wrap the Address input and Analyze Deal button in a shared row container (e.g., <div class="deal-form-row">...).

Apply flex-based layout: display:flex; gap:12px (or project spacing token); align-items:flex-end (or baseline if labels/inputs align better); make the input container flex:1 1 auto; min-width:0; keep the button auto width.
Add a mobile breakpoint to stack vertically: flex-direction:column; align-items:stretch; make the button width:100% on small screens.
Remove any ad-hoc margins between the input and button; rely on gap/spacing tokens for consistency.
Ensure keyboard order remains Label -> Address input -> Analyze button; verify button has accessible name (the visible text is sufficient).
Test across breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop), major browsers, and with long addresses to ensure no overflow or wrapping issues.

If the project uses a utility framework (e.g., Tailwind/Bootstrap), implement with existing utility classes instead of custom CSS; otherwise, add minimal scoped CSS near the component or in the screen stylesheet.
notes:
Example Tailwind:
[Address Field]
Use existing design tokens for spacing, sizes, and colors; avoid inline styles and magic numbers.
Do not alter button click behavior or introduce new state; this is a layout-only change.

handoff.next_required_action: Implement the layout change on the Deals & Scenarios screen using the recommended flex row structure and responsive behavior, then open a PR for review.
